details.description: PhD in computational musicology received at the Maria Curie-Sklodowska University in Lublin (Poland)
Master in composition received at the National Academy of Music n. Piotr Tchaikovsky in Kyiv (Ukraine)
Licentiate in composition received at the National Academy of Music n. Piotr Tchaikovsky in Kyiv (Ukraine)
Organist diploma of Sacral Music Institute in Przemyśl (Poland)
Pierre Lamy concert hall in Annecy (France)
Concert hall of the Associazione Europea di Musica e Comunicazione (Montecassiano, Italy)
Large and Small concert halls of the National Academy of Music n. P. Tchaikovsky in Kyiv (Ukraine)
Concert hall of the Lithuanian Union of Composers (Vilnius, Lithuania)
Concert hall of the Academic Center of Culture of Maria Curie-Skłodowska University (Lublin, Poland)
St. Alexander Cathedral in Kyiv
Neue Apostolische Kirche in Kyiv
Small concert hall of the High Institut of Music n. R. Glier in Kyiv
Concert hall of Lesia Ukrainka Museum in Kyiv
Concert hall of the Centrum of Culture in Nova Kachovka (Ukraine)
Concert hall of Music College in Kryvyi Rih (Ukraine)
Concert hall of the Children's Academy of Arts in Kyiv
Prize in category Culture et Arts Numériques by VRJAM 2023 for the "Volumetric music composition in the VR" project (Toulouse, France)
Winner of the International composition competition "Vivat musica!" (Nova Kachovka, Ukraine)
Laureate of the Ukrainian composition competition n. Karabitz (Kyiv, Ukraine)
Finalist of the International composition competition "CIMFA" (Annecy, France)
Prize for the best instrumental work received at the regional composition competition (Kryvyi Rih, Ukraine)
"Soeurs Jumelles" music festival (2024, Rochefort, France)
"Soeurs Jumelles" music festival (2023, Rochefort, France)
"Music, Communication and Performance" conference-festival (2018, Montecassiano, Italy)
"KULturalia" art festival (2015, Lublin, Poland)
"XVII Lubelskie Forum Sztuki Współczesnej im. Witolta Lutosławskiego" music festival (2013, Lublin, Poland)
"The night of culture" art festival (2013, Lublin, Poland)
"Kyiv's premiers of the season" music festival (2004, Kyiv, Ukraine)
Shvets, A. At the Stream. In Polyphony of Living; Field Studies Council (UK): London, 2001; pp 90-91
Shvets, A. Piece for Flute and Piano. In Works of Young Composers – Winners of the III International Competition "Vivat, Musica!"; Energomasz: Nova Kachovka, 2004, pp 28-30
31 scientific publication in scientific peer-reviewed journals and books, which are included to the Web of Science, Scopus, Google Scholar and other systems of indexing.

Active participation in 30 international conferences and scientific workshops in Washington, Boston, London, Paris, Copenhagen, Bordeaux, Sophia Antipolis, Salzburg, Florence, Vilnius, Kraków and other European cities.
Selection committee member at international conferences and Open Access journals in France and UK. More detailed information about research activity can be found at shvetscomposer.wordpress.com

Masterclass on baroque and romantic organ music performance given by the rector of Hochschule für Katholische Kirchenmusik und Musikpädagogik Regensburg Stefan Baier (Lublin 2013, active participation).
Masterclass on baroque and romantic organ music performance given by the professor of Karol Szymanowski Academy of Music in Katowice Marek Toporowski (Katowice 2008, active participation).
Masterclass on renaissance organ music performance given by the professor of Scuola di Musica Santa Cecilia Christopher Stembridge (Kiev 2008, passive participation).
Masterclass on renaissance choir music performance given by professors of Schola Cantorum Basiliensis (Kiev 2007, active participation).
